summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the default JustHTML sanitizer strips normal remote image URLs but preserves backslash-prefixed variants that a WHATWG URL resolver upgrades to `https://evil.test/track`.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that default `img[src]` sanitization blocks `https://evil.test/track` and `//evil.test/track`, but preserves `\\evil.test/track` and `/\\evil.test/track`, which a browser-style WHATWG resolver converts into `https://evil.test/track`.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned `https://github.com/EmilStenstrom/justhtml.git` at `c5185651a443fb806343e6e5b081b49bab588d21`, built the Docker image, and produced: `CONTROL_ABSOLUTE=`, `CONTROL_PROTOCOL_RELATIVE=`, `BYPASS_ONE_HTML=`, `BYPASS_ONE_RESOLVED=https://evil.test/track`, `BYPASS_TWO_HTML=`, `BYPASS_TWO_RESOLVED=https://evil.test/track`, followed by the success message.
verification_method: The helper script exercises the default sanitizer on both blocked controls and backslash-prefixed bypass inputs, extracts the surviving `src` values, and resolves them with Node's WHATWG `URL` implementation against `https://victim.test/view`. The run only succeeds if the controls are stripped and the surviving values resolve cross-origin.
evidence_produced_by_run: The absolute and protocol-relative control cases both sanitized to `` with no `src` attribute.
The first bypass case sanitized to `` and resolved to `https://evil.test/track`.
The second bypass case sanitized to `` and resolved to `https://evil.test/track`.
The container printed `SUCCESS: default relative-only img[src] policy preserved backslash-prefixed values that resolve cross-origin`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
